contract.goal: Port .claude/scripts/guild/findings.ts (subverbs: append + count) to .claude/cli/verbs/guild/findings.ts. Byte-equivalent behavior. Both subverbs read/write projects/<slug>/.guild-findings.jsonl via ctx.cwd. D2 of 5 in Phase 2.
acceptance_criteria: findingsVerb(rest, ctx) exported; dispatches on rest[0] to append or count subverb.

append: writes JSONL row {ts, slug, branch, unit, evaluator, code, signature, evidence, severity} to <cwd>/projects/<slug>/.guild-findings.jsonl. Signature is sha1 of evaluator+code+normalizedEvidence.
count: returns count of rows matching the computed signature. '0' if file missing. Skips malformed JSONL rows.
All legacy error paths preserved (missing args, invalid severity, missing project dir, unknown/missing subverb).
15 sibling tests pass. Byte-parity confirmed via diff against legacy.
rules_applied: Mirror Phase 1 / Phase 2 D1 <verb>Verb naming convention.
ValidationError class for cross-function flow (matches the pattern from mediate-panel + parse-and-aggregate).
Subverb helpers renamed appendSubverb/countSubverb to avoid colliding with the verb-handler suffix.
disqualifiers: Change JSONL row schema, signature algorithm, or stdout text.
Change the JSONL file location or filename.

actions: Wrote .claude/cli/verbs/guild/findings.ts (220 lines, ports legacy with ValidationError class; helpers renamed appendSubverb/countSubverb).
Registered 'findings' in GUILD_VERBS map and VERBS help registry.
Wrote findings.test.ts (15 tests adapted from legacy).
Verified parity: bin/guild findings append/count vs node .claude/scripts/guild/findings.ts append/count — same signature, same row content, same count output.
Ran npm test: 37 files / 496 tests pass.
Spawned evaluator-contract-fit + evaluator-naming in parallel. Both approved.
corrections: Line-number citations in evaluator packets continue to drift (cited 218-235 / 223-235 when actual file ends at 214). Same friction as Phase 1 D3. Recurring L-013 violation. Fix: cite ranges only after a fresh Read.

notes_for_pr: D2 of 5 in Phase 2. PR refresh at phase close.
Byte-for-byte parity verified for both subverbs via diff against legacy script.

Naming evaluator advisory observation (not blocking): the codebase now has 3 sibling ValidationError classes (mediate-panel, parse-and-aggregate, findings). Same name, same concept; appropriate to keep separate for module-local catch flow. Worth a panel-level consolidation thought if a future cross-module catch ever materializes — not D2's scope.
Recurring L-013 friction: cited line numbers from memory; off by 5-10 lines. Next packet should cite ranges only after a fresh Read.
